svg.text: Parquet read & decode path in DuckDB — remote (cache_httpfs) vs. local vs. proposed unikernel cache
DuckDB v1.5.4 + duck-read-cache-fs · all caches below layer 2 hold raw compressed bytes — decoding is redone every scan · ⚠ = implicit cache that silently warms baselines · ★ = proposed
ParquetScanImplementation (parquet_extension.cpp) pulls one DataChunk (≤ 2048 rows) per call · row groups distributed across threads
ParquetReader::InitializeScan / Scan (parquet_reader.cpp:1290) · one ColumnReader tree per file
2 · Column decode — ColumnReader (column_reader.cpp) — CPU work, above all byte caches
Footer: ThriftFileTransport decodes thrift metadata → row-group / column-chunk offsets (⚠ parquet_metadata_cache keeps parsed footers, default off)
Per column chunk: fetch page bytes → DecompressInternal — snappy / zstd / gzip / lz4 / brotli (column_reader.cpp:409)
→ decode PLAIN / DICTIONARY / RLE / DELTA (decoder/) → filter pushdown & row-id selection → DuckDB Vectors
Decoded pages are never cached — every scan pays decode again ⇒ warm-run floor ≈ decode CPU time; no byte cache can go below it
PrepareRowGroupBuffer (parquet_reader.cpp:1192) registers needed column-chunk byte ranges → merges ranges with gaps ≤ 16 KiB → Prefetch()
Footer read sized by guess: file_size/1000, clamped to [16 KiB, 256 KiB] — saves remote round-trips
Prefetch is ON for remote files, OFF for local (unless prefetch_all_parquet_files) · disable via disable_parquet_prefetching
no OS page cache in this path
Parquet always opens via CachingFileSystem::Get (parquet_reader.cpp:871), local and remote · full hit = zero-copy (pinned buffer)
Byte ranges pinned in BufferManager memory · gap-fill merge ≤ 1 MiB · validated per open (mtime / ETag)
Shares memory_limit with the query; evicted first under pressure (dropped, re-read)
Disabled by cache_httpfs at load — SET enable_external_file_cache=true to re-enable
5 · VirtualFileSystem::FindFileSystem(path) — dispatch by CanHandleFile(); cache_httpfs swaps in its wrapper
metadata LRU (no HEAD) · file-handle LRU (no re-open/TLS) · glob LRU (no LIST)
ReadImpl (:602): align request to 512 KiB blocks → one parallel task per block
(thread pool or DuckDB TaskScheduler) · exclusion regexes bypass cache
in_mem: L1 only · noop: pass-through · ⚠ L2 re-reads are served via OS page cache
SigV4 sign → HTTP GET Range: bytes=off–end (httpfs.cpp:287) · LIFO conn pool
PARALLEL_ACCESS ⇒ httpfs' own 1–32 MiB read buffer is skipped
⚠ httpfs-internal metadata cache + full-download-below-threshold path stay active
Object storage — S3 / GCS / HTTP (network, egress-billed)
every pread() fills the page cache;

g.text: reader at layer 7a: on_disk / in_mem / noop (pure httpfs)
block alignment = fetch granularity = cache key (read amplification!)
max parallel range-GETs issued per logical read
extension's own pool vs. DuckDB TaskScheduler
per-block ETag check; default is TTL-based eventual consistency
dedup of HEAD / open+TLS / LIST requests (layer 6a)
disk-space guard before writing L2 block files
L2 eviction: stale-after-1-day vs. in-process LRU (lru_sp)
keeps the L1 in-memory tier above disk blocks (+ O_DIRECT reads)
toggles layer-4 byte-range cache (cache_httpfs turns it off at load)
ETag / mtime check on every open (staleness → drop ranges)
shared BufferPool budget; external-file ranges are evicted first
turns off the layer-3 ReadAheadBuffer entirely
enables layer-3 prefetch for local files too (default: remote only)
caches parsed footers — skips thrift re-decode at layer 2

svg.text: File references relative to duck-read-cache-fs/ (extension: src/ · DuckDB core: duckdb/src/, duckdb/extension/parquet/ · httpfs: duckdb-httpfs/src/). ★ = proposed, not in codebase. Generated 2026-07-22.
